title: Aetheolaena otophora
intro: Aetheolaena otophora, also known as 'Chiraito', is a plant species that belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly used in traditional medicine, particularly in Nepal and parts of India. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Aetheolaena otophora include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is typically used to make various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as essential oils, which are used in traditional medicine and aromatherapy.
taxonomy: Aetheolaena otophora is a species within the plant family Asteraceae. It is a member of the tribe Tanaaceae and the genus Aetheolaena. Further classification places it in the subtribe Tanaidinae. This species is native to the tropical regions of South America. Its classification is part of a broader system of plant taxonomy.
